title: Module 6.1: FAA Regulations for Laser Operations
description: Comprehensive guide to FAA regulations governing outdoor laser operations affecting navigable airspace, including filing requirements, review processes, and compliance obligations.

learning_objectives.objective: Explain FAA's authority to regulate outdoor laser operations under 49 U.S.C. § 40103
Complete FAA Form 7140-1 with accurate laser configuration data
Navigate the FAA review timeline and understand determination outcomes
Apply 18 USC 39A provisions to laser-aviation incidents

sections.title: FAA Regulatory Authority
sections.content_summary: Overview of FAA's statutory authority, relationship with FDA/CDRH, and the regulatory framework for outdoor laser operations affecting navigable airspace.
key_concepts: 49 U.S.C. § 40103 - Sovereignty and Use of Airspace

sections.title: NOTAM Filing Simulator
sections.content_summary: Interactive Form 7140-1 walkthrough with field-by-field guidance, validation, and submission simulation.

sections.title: FAA Review Process
sections.content_summary: Visual timeline of the FAA review process from initial submission to Letter of Determination issuance.

sections.title: Legal Framework & Enforcement
sections.content_summary: 18 USC 39A criminal provisions, civil penalties under 49 U.S.C. § 46301, and real-world enforcement case studies.

questions.question: How many days before a planned outdoor laser operation should Form 7140-1 be filed with FAA?
questions.explanation: FAA requests proponents file a notice at least 30 days before the planned event to allow sufficient time for aeronautical study and determination.

questions.question: Which federal law specifically makes aiming a laser pointer at an aircraft a federal crime?
questions.explanation: 18 USC 39A makes it illegal to aim a laser pointer at an aircraft or its flight path, with penalties up to 5 years in prison and $250,000 fine.

questions.question: What is the maximum civil penalty FAA may seek per violation for aiming a laser at an aircraft?
questions.explanation: Under 49 U.S.C. § 46301(a)(5)(A), FAA may seek a maximum civil penalty of $11,000 per violation.
